questions.question: Explain the purpose of the 'else' clause in a try-except block in Python. When is the else block executed? Provide a code example demonstrating its usage.
keyPoints: Else block executes only when no exception occurs
Executes after try block completes successfully
Used for code that should run when no errors were raised
Distinguishes between exceptional and normal execution flow

questions.question: Create a custom exception class in Python named 'InvalidInputError' that inherits from the Exception base class. Write a function that raises this exception when user input is invalid (e.g., negative number for age), and demonstrate how to handle it with an except block.
keyPoints: Custom exceptions inherit from Exception class
Use 'raise' keyword to throw custom exceptions
Can add custom attributes and messages
Try-except catches custom exceptions like built-in ones

questions.question: Write a Python program that demonstrates handling multiple exceptions in a single try block using multiple 'except' clauses. Handle at least three different exception types (e.g., FileNotFoundError, ValueError, ZeroDivisionError) with specific error messages for each.
keyPoints: Multiple except blocks catch different exception types
Specific exceptions should come before general ones
Each except can have its own error handling logic
Order matters - more specific exceptions first

questions.question: List and briefly explain at least five common built-in exceptions in Python used in exception handling (e.g., ValueError, TypeError, IndexError, KeyError, AttributeError). Provide one example scenario where each exception would be raised.
keyPoints: IndexError: sequence index out of range
AttributeError: attribute reference or assignment failure
